title: Stenostelma corniculatum
taxonomy: Stenostelma corniculatum belongs to the family Apocynaceae. This family comprises a wide range of plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander. Within Apocynaceae, Stenostelma corniculatum is further classified as a species of the genus Stenostelma. The genus Stenostelma is a small group of flowering plants found primarily in southern Africa. These plants are known for their unique characteristics and adaptations to their native environments.

intro: Stenostelma corniculatum, also known as Devil's Claw, is a perennial plant species native to southern Africa, characterized by its claw-shaped roots and succulent leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and pain, as well as alleviating symptoms of arthritis, digestive issues, and anxiety. The bioactive constituents of Stenostelma corniculatum include iridoid glycosides, particularly harpagoside, which is believed to be responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or capsule, with the roots often being used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions.
